purpose: P0-06 — 영어 문법 6 anchor × 5 도구 = 30 seed (PoC). Phase 1 5문제 ≥ 70% 검증용. trigger 라이브러리 일반화 (수학 6 anchor 모델) 의 1차 적용.

tools.name: 현재완료 vs 단순과거 (since/for vs ago)
tools.tool_proposition: since/for + 기간이 보이면 현재완료, ago 가 보이면 단순과거를 쓴다.

triggers.ko: since/for + 기간이 보이면 현재완료를 쓴다
triggers.en: If you see 'since' or 'for' with a duration, use present perfect (have/has + p.p.).

triggers.ko: ago 가 보이면 단순과거를 쓴다
triggers.en: If you see 'ago', use simple past (V-ed), not present perfect.

triggers.ko: have/has + ago 조합은 오류
triggers.en: The combination 'have/has ... ago' is grammatically incorrect.

tools.common_mistake_example: I have met him three years ago. (X) → I met him three years ago. (O)

tools.name: 시간/조건 부사절 미래 표현
tools.tool_proposition: when/if/before/after/until 부사절에서는 미래시제(will)를 쓰지 않고 현재시제로 미래를 표현한다.

triggers.ko: when/if/before/after/until 부사절은 will 쓰지 않는다
triggers.en: In adverbial clauses with when/if/before/after/until, do NOT use 'will'.

triggers.ko: 시간/조건 부사절에서 미래는 현재시제로 표현한다
triggers.en: Express future time with present tense in time/condition clauses.

triggers.ko: 주절은 will 가능, 부사절은 현재시제
triggers.en: Main clause may use 'will', but the time/condition clause uses present tense.

tools.common_mistake_example: I will call you when I will arrive. (X) → I will call you when I arrive. (O)

tools.name: 진행형 불가 동사 (state verbs)
tools.tool_proposition: know/believe/love/hate/own/belong 같은 상태동사는 진행형(be V-ing)으로 쓰지 않는다.

triggers.ko: know/believe/love/own 같은 상태동사는 진행형 X
triggers.en: State verbs (know, believe, love, own, belong) cannot be used in progressive form.

triggers.ko: 감각·인지·감정·소유 동사는 단순현재로
triggers.en: Verbs of perception, cognition, emotion, possession use simple tenses, not progressive.

triggers.ko: I am knowing, He is owning 은 오류
triggers.en: Phrases like 'I am knowing' or 'He is owning' are incorrect.

tools.common_mistake_example: I am knowing the answer. (X) → I know the answer. (O)

tools.name: 시제 일치 (간접화법)
tools.tool_proposition: 주절이 과거시제이면 종속절은 한 단계 과거로 시제를 일치시킨다 (현재→과거, 과거→과거완료).

triggers.ko: 주절이 said/told 같은 과거이면 종속절도 시제 한 단계 과거로
triggers.en: If the main verb is past (said/told), shift the subordinate clause one step into the past.

triggers.ko: 현재시제 → 과거시제, 현재완료/과거 → 과거완료
triggers.en: Present → past; present perfect or past → past perfect.

triggers.ko: 보편적 진리는 시제 일치 예외 (현재시제 유지)
triggers.en: Universal truths stay in present tense (exception to backshift).

tools.common_mistake_example: He said he is happy. (X) → He said he was happy. (O)

tools.name: 미래 진행/완료 (will be V-ing / will have p.p.)
tools.tool_proposition: 특정 미래 시점의 진행 동작은 will be V-ing, 그 시점까지 완료된 상태는 will have p.p. 로 표현한다.

triggers.ko: 특정 미래 시점에 진행 중인 동작은 will be V-ing
triggers.en: Use 'will be V-ing' for an action in progress at a specific future time.

triggers.ko: 특정 미래 시점까지 완료된 상태는 will have p.p.
triggers.en: Use 'will have p.p.' for an action completed by a specific future time.

triggers.ko: by + 미래시점 + will have p.p. 결합 패턴
triggers.en: Pattern: 'by + future time + will have p.p.'

tools.common_mistake_example: By next year, I will study English for 10 years. (X) → By next year, I will have studied English for 10 years. (O)

tools.name: 주격 who vs 목적격 whom
tools.tool_proposition: 선행사가 사람이고 관계절 안에서 주어 역할이면 who, 목적어 역할이면 whom.

triggers.ko: 선행사가 사람 + 관계절 안에서 주어 자리면 who
triggers.en: Use 'who' when the antecedent is a person and serves as subject in the relative clause.

triggers.ko: 선행사가 사람 + 관계절 안에서 목적어 자리면 whom
triggers.en: Use 'whom' when the antecedent is a person and serves as object in the relative clause.

triggers.ko: 회화에서는 whom 대신 who 도 자주 쓰이지만 시험에서는 whom
triggers.en: In formal/exam contexts, use 'whom' for object position even though spoken English often uses 'who'.

tools.common_mistake_example: The man who I met yesterday. (informal OK) → The man whom I met yesterday. (formal/exam)

tools.name: that vs which
tools.tool_proposition: 한정적 용법(콤마 X)에는 that/which 둘 다 가능하지만, 계속적 용법(콤마)에는 which 만 가능하고 that 은 쓸 수 없다.

triggers.ko: 콤마 + 관계대명사 = which (that 불가)
triggers.en: After a comma (non-restrictive clause), use 'which', not 'that'.

triggers.ko: 한정적 용법 (콤마 없음) 에는 that/which 둘 다 가능
triggers.en: In restrictive clauses (no comma), both 'that' and 'which' are acceptable.

triggers.ko: , that 패턴이 보이면 오류 → , which 로 수정
triggers.en: If you see ', that' it is an error → change to ', which'.

tools.common_mistake_example: My car, that is red, is fast. (X) → My car, which is red, is fast. (O)

tools.name: 관계부사 (where/when/why/how)
tools.tool_proposition: 선행사가 장소·시간·이유·방법이고 관계절이 완전한 문장이면 관계부사를 쓴다 (전치사+관계대명사 대체).

triggers.ko: 선행사 = 장소 + 관계절이 완전한 문장 → where
triggers.en: Antecedent is a place + complete clause follows → use 'where'.

triggers.ko: 선행사 = 시간 + 관계절이 완전한 문장 → when
triggers.en: Antecedent is a time + complete clause follows → use 'when'.

triggers.ko: 관계대명사 뒤는 불완전, 관계부사 뒤는 완전
triggers.en: After a relative pronoun the clause is incomplete; after a relative adverb the clause is complete.

tools.common_mistake_example: The house which I live is big. (X) → The house where I live is big. (O)

tools.name: 관계대명사 계속적 용법 (콤마)
tools.tool_proposition: 콤마 + 관계대명사는 추가 정보(부가 설명)를 제공하며, 관계절을 빼도 문장이 성립한다. that 사용 불가.

triggers.ko: 콤마 + 관계대명사 = 부가 정보 (생략 가능)
triggers.en: Comma + relative pronoun adds extra info that can be omitted without breaking the sentence.

triggers.ko: 계속적 용법은 that 사용 불가, which/who 만 가능
triggers.en: Non-restrictive clauses cannot use 'that' — only 'which' or 'who'.

triggers.ko: 선행사가 고유명사면 보통 계속적 용법
triggers.en: Proper nouns typically take non-restrictive (comma) clauses.

tools.common_mistake_example: Seoul, that is the capital of Korea, ... (X) → Seoul, which is the capital of Korea, ... (O)

tools.name: 소유격 whose
tools.tool_proposition: 선행사의 소유 관계를 나타낼 때 whose 를 쓴다 (선행사가 사람·사물 모두 가능).

triggers.ko: 선행사 + 소유물 관계 → whose + 명사
triggers.en: When the antecedent owns the noun in the relative clause, use 'whose + noun'.

triggers.ko: whose 는 사람·사물 선행사 모두 가능
triggers.en: 'Whose' can be used for both human and non-human antecedents.

triggers.ko: of which 도 가능하지만 어색함 (whose 권장)
triggers.en: 'Of which' is grammatical but awkward; 'whose' is preferred.

tools.common_mistake_example: The boy who his name is Tom. (X) → The boy whose name is Tom. (O)

tools.name: 가정법 과거 (현재 사실의 반대)
tools.tool_proposition: 현재 사실의 반대 가정은 if + 과거시제, 주절은 would/could/might + 동사원형.

triggers.ko: if + 과거시제 / would + 동사원형 = 현재 사실 반대
triggers.en: Pattern: 'if + past tense, ... would + base verb' = contrary to present fact.

triggers.ko: if I were (was X) — 가정법은 항상 were
triggers.en: In the subjunctive, use 'were' for all subjects (e.g., 'if I were', not 'if I was').

triggers.ko: 주절 will 은 오류 (가정법 과거에서는 would)
triggers.en: Do not use 'will' in the main clause of a past subjunctive — use 'would'.

tools.common_mistake_example: If I was rich, I will buy a car. (X) → If I were rich, I would buy a car. (O)

tools.name: 가정법 과거완료 (과거 사실의 반대)
tools.tool_proposition: 과거 사실의 반대 가정은 if + had p.p., 주절은 would/could/might have p.p.

triggers.ko: if + had p.p. / would have p.p. = 과거 사실 반대
triggers.en: Pattern: 'if + had p.p., ... would have p.p.' = contrary to past fact.

triggers.ko: 주절 would + 동사원형 은 가정법 과거 (과거완료 X)
triggers.en: 'would + base verb' is past subjunctive (present), not past perfect subjunctive.

triggers.ko: 과거의 후회·반사실은 항상 had p.p. + would have p.p.
triggers.en: Past regret or counter-fact always uses 'had p.p.' + 'would have p.p.'

tools.common_mistake_example: If I had studied harder, I would pass. (X) → If I had studied harder, I would have passed. (O)

tools.name: I wish + 가정법
tools.tool_proposition: I wish 다음 절이 현재 반대 소망이면 과거시제, 과거 반대 후회면 had p.p.

triggers.ko: I wish + 과거시제 = 현재 소망 (지금 그렇지 않다)
triggers.en: 'I wish + past tense' expresses a present unfulfilled wish.

triggers.ko: I wish + had p.p. = 과거 후회 (그때 그렇지 않았다)
triggers.en: 'I wish + had p.p.' expresses regret about a past event.

triggers.ko: I wish + 현재시제 는 일반적으로 오류
triggers.en: 'I wish + present tense' is usually incorrect for unfulfilled wishes.

tools.common_mistake_example: I wish I am taller. (X) → I wish I were taller. (O)

tools.name: as if / as though + 가정법
tools.tool_proposition: as if/though 다음에 과거시제는 현재 반사실, had p.p. 는 과거 반사실.

triggers.ko: as if + 과거시제 = 현재 반사실 (마치 ~인 것처럼)
triggers.en: 'as if + past tense' = contrary to present reality.

triggers.ko: as if + had p.p. = 과거 반사실
triggers.en: 'as if + had p.p.' = contrary to past reality.

triggers.ko: as if 다음 직설법 현재시제도 가능 (사실에 가까울 때)
triggers.en: Indicative present after 'as if' is possible when speaker considers it close to fact.

tools.common_mistake_example: He talks as if he is a doctor. (가정 의도) → He talks as if he were a doctor. (O)

tools.name: 혼합 가정법 (mixed conditional)
tools.tool_proposition: 과거의 원인이 현재까지 영향을 미치는 가정은 if + had p.p. / would + 동사원형 (혼합).

triggers.ko: if + had p.p. / would + 동사원형 = 과거 원인 + 현재 결과
triggers.en: 'if + had p.p., would + base' = past cause + present result.

triggers.ko: if 절이 과거 사실 반대인데 주절이 현재 결과면 mixed
triggers.en: When the if-clause is past counter-fact and the main clause refers to present, use mixed conditional.

triggers.ko: now / today 같은 현재 부사가 주절에 있으면 mixed 신호
triggers.en: Adverbs like 'now' or 'today' in the main clause signal a mixed conditional.

tools.common_mistake_example: If I had studied medicine, I would have been a doctor now. (X) → ... I would be a doctor now. (O)

tools.name: 기본 수동태 (be + p.p.)
tools.tool_proposition: 능동의 목적어가 주어가 되고, 동사는 be + p.p., 행위자는 by + 행위자로 표현한다.

triggers.ko: 타동사 + 목적어 → 목적어가 주어, be + p.p.
triggers.en: Active object becomes passive subject; verb becomes 'be + p.p.'

triggers.ko: 행위자 강조 시 by + 행위자, 불필요 시 생략
triggers.en: Add 'by + agent' when relevant; omit when the agent is unimportant.

triggers.ko: 시제는 be 동사로 표현 (was/were/is/are/has been)
triggers.en: Tense in passive is shown via the be-verb (was, is, has been, etc.).

tools.common_mistake_example: The book wrote by him. (X) → The book was written by him. (O)

tools.name: 4형식 수동태 (간접목적어 to/for)
tools.tool_proposition: 4형식 수동태에서 직접목적어를 주어로 만들면 간접목적어 앞에 to/for 전치사가 필요하다.

triggers.ko: give/send/teach 류 동사는 to + 간접목적어
triggers.en: Verbs like give/send/teach take 'to + indirect object' in passive.

triggers.ko: make/buy/find 류 동사는 for + 간접목적어
triggers.en: Verbs like make/buy/find take 'for + indirect object' in passive.

triggers.ko: 직접목적어 주어 변환 시 전치사 필수
triggers.en: When the direct object becomes subject, a preposition (to/for) must precede the indirect object.

tools.common_mistake_example: A book was given me. (X) → A book was given to me. (O)

tools.name: 5형식 수동태 (목적격 보어)
tools.tool_proposition: 5형식의 목적어를 수동태 주어로 바꾸면 목적격 보어는 그 자리에 그대로 남고, 사역·지각동사는 to부정사로 변환된다.

triggers.ko: 5형식 수동태 — 목적격 보어는 주격 보어로 그대로
triggers.en: In passive of 5-pattern verbs, the object complement remains as subject complement.

triggers.ko: 사역동사 (make/let/have) 수동태 — 원형부정사 → to부정사
triggers.en: With causative verbs (make/let/have) in passive, bare infinitive becomes to-infinitive.

triggers.ko: 지각동사 (see/hear/feel) 수동태 — 원형부정사 → to부정사
triggers.en: With perception verbs (see/hear/feel) in passive, bare infinitive becomes to-infinitive.

tools.common_mistake_example: He was made study. (X) → He was made to study. (O)

tools.name: by 외 전치사 (감정 형용사 패턴)
tools.tool_proposition: interested in / surprised at / satisfied with 같은 감정 표현은 by 가 아닌 고유 전치사를 쓴다.

triggers.ko: interested + in (about 아님)
triggers.en: 'Interested' takes 'in', not 'about' or 'by'.

triggers.ko: surprised/shocked + at/by 둘 다 가능
triggers.en: 'Surprised/shocked' can take 'at' or 'by'.

triggers.ko: satisfied/pleased + with
triggers.en: 'Satisfied/pleased' takes 'with'.

tools.common_mistake_example: I am interested by music. (X) → I am interested in music. (O)

tools.name: 자동사는 수동태 불가
tools.tool_proposition: happen/occur/disappear/arrive 같은 자동사는 목적어가 없으므로 수동태로 변환할 수 없다.

triggers.ko: happen/occur 등 자동사는 수동태 X
triggers.en: Intransitive verbs (happen, occur, disappear, arrive) have no passive form.

triggers.ko: be happened, was occurred 는 항상 오류
triggers.en: Phrases like 'be happened' or 'was occurred' are always incorrect.

triggers.ko: 능동으로만 표현 (사건이 발생했다 = the event happened)
triggers.en: Express such events only in active voice ('the event happened').

tools.common_mistake_example: An accident was happened yesterday. (X) → An accident happened yesterday. (O)

tools.name: 현재분사(능동) vs 과거분사(수동)
tools.tool_proposition: 수식하는 명사가 행위 주체이면 현재분사(V-ing), 행위 대상이면 과거분사(V-ed).

triggers.ko: 명사가 행위 주체 (능동) → V-ing
triggers.en: If the noun performs the action (active), use V-ing.

triggers.ko: 명사가 행위 대상 (수동) → V-ed (p.p.)
triggers.en: If the noun receives the action (passive), use V-ed (past participle).

triggers.ko: 감정 동사: 사람은 V-ed, 사물은 V-ing
triggers.en: Emotion verbs: people take V-ed (interested), things take V-ing (interesting).

tools.common_mistake_example: I am boring. (X, '나는 지루한 사람') → I am bored. (O, '나는 지루함을 느낀다')

tools.name: 분사구문 (시간/이유/조건)
tools.tool_proposition: 부사절의 접속사+주어를 생략하고 동사를 V-ing 또는 (Being) p.p. 로 변환한다. 주어는 주절과 일치해야 한다.

triggers.ko: 분사구문은 주어가 주절과 일치해야 한다
triggers.en: The implicit subject of a participle phrase must match the main clause subject.

triggers.ko: 능동 의미면 V-ing, 수동 의미면 (Being) p.p.
triggers.en: Use V-ing for active meaning, (Being) p.p. for passive meaning.

triggers.ko: 주어 다르면 독립분사구문 (with + 명사 + 분사)
triggers.en: If subjects differ, use absolute participle ('with + noun + participle').

tools.common_mistake_example: Walking down the street, the rain fell. (X, 비가 걷지 않음) → Walking down the street, I felt the rain. (O)

tools.name: 동명사 vs to부정사 목적어
tools.tool_proposition: enjoy/finish/avoid/mind/quit 은 동명사만, want/decide/hope/promise 는 to부정사만 목적어로 취한다.

triggers.ko: enjoy/finish/avoid/mind/quit + V-ing (to부정사 X)
triggers.en: Verbs like enjoy/finish/avoid/mind/quit take only gerund (V-ing), not to-infinitive.

triggers.ko: want/decide/hope/promise + to V (V-ing X)
triggers.en: Verbs like want/decide/hope/promise take only to-infinitive, not gerund.

triggers.ko: remember/forget/stop/try 는 의미에 따라 둘 다 가능 (의미 차이)
triggers.en: Remember/forget/stop/try take both, but with different meanings.

tools.common_mistake_example: I enjoy to swim. (X) → I enjoy swimming. (O)

tools.name: 완료분사 (having + p.p.)
tools.tool_proposition: 분사가 나타내는 시점이 주절보다 먼저면 having + p.p. (완료분사) 를 쓴다.

triggers.ko: 분사 시점 > 주절 시점 → having + p.p.
triggers.en: If the participle action precedes the main clause, use 'having + p.p.'

triggers.ko: 단순 V-ing 은 주절과 동시 동작
triggers.en: Simple V-ing implies the action is simultaneous with the main clause.

triggers.ko: Having p.p. 는 보통 시간/이유 분사구문
triggers.en: 'Having p.p.' typically marks time-precedence or reason in participle phrases.

tools.common_mistake_example: Finishing my homework, I had played soccer. (X, 시제 전후 모호) → Having finished my homework, I played soccer. (O)

tools.name: with + 명사 + 분사 (부대상황)
tools.tool_proposition: 부대상황을 나타낼 때 with + 명사 + V-ing(능동)/V-ed(수동) 패턴을 쓴다.

triggers.ko: with + 명사 + V-ing — 능동 부대상황
triggers.en: 'with + noun + V-ing' shows an accompanying active state.

triggers.ko: with + 명사 + V-ed (p.p.) — 수동 부대상황
triggers.en: 'with + noun + V-ed' shows an accompanying passive state.

triggers.ko: 주어 일치 불요 (독립분사 변형)
triggers.en: Subjects need not match the main clause (absolute construction).

tools.common_mistake_example: He stood with his eyes closing. (X, 의미 모호) → He stood with his eyes closed. (O)

tools.name: 도치 (부정어 문두 / Only)
tools.tool_proposition: Never/Rarely/Hardly/Not only/Only 같은 부정·제한 표현이 문두에 오면 주어-동사 도치가 일어난다.

triggers.ko: Never/Rarely/Hardly + 문두 → 도치 (조동사 + 주어)
triggers.en: Never/Rarely/Hardly at sentence start triggers inversion (auxiliary + subject).

triggers.ko: Not only + 문두 → 도치 + but also 호응
triggers.en: 'Not only' fronted triggers inversion and pairs with 'but also'.

triggers.ko: Only + 부사구 + 문두 → 도치
triggers.en: 'Only + adverbial' fronted triggers inversion.

tools.common_mistake_example: Never I have seen such a sight. (X) → Never have I seen such a sight. (O)

tools.name: 강조구문 (It is ~ that)
tools.tool_proposition: 강조하려는 부분을 It is/was 와 that 사이에 넣어 강조하며, 강조 대상에 따라 that 대신 who/which 도 가능.

triggers.ko: It is/was + 강조어구 + that ... — 강조구문
triggers.en: Cleft pattern: 'It is/was + emphasized phrase + that ...'

triggers.ko: 강조 대상이 사람이면 that 대신 who 가능
triggers.en: If the emphasized phrase is a person, 'who' may replace 'that'.

triggers.ko: 강조 대상이 시간·장소면 when/where 도 가능
triggers.en: If emphasizing time or place, 'when' or 'where' may replace 'that'.

tools.common_mistake_example: It is yesterday I met him. (X) → It is yesterday that I met him. (O)

tools.name: 동격 that vs 관계대명사 that
tools.tool_proposition: 선행사가 fact/idea/news 같은 추상명사이고 뒤 절이 완전하면 동격 that, 불완전하면 관계대명사 that.

triggers.ko: fact/idea/news + that + 완전한 문장 = 동격
triggers.en: Abstract noun (fact/idea/news) + 'that' + complete clause = appositive.

triggers.ko: 선행사 + that + 불완전한 문장 = 관계대명사
triggers.en: Antecedent + 'that' + incomplete clause = relative pronoun.

triggers.ko: 동격 that 는 의미상 선행사 = 절 (선행사를 풀어쓴 것)
triggers.en: Appositive 'that' equates the noun with the clause itself (re-statement).

tools.common_mistake_example: The fact which he is honest is clear. (X, fact 는 동격 명사) → The fact that he is honest is clear. (O)

tools.name: 가주어 it / 진주어 to V·that 절
tools.tool_proposition: 주어가 너무 길면 it 을 가주어로 두고 to부정사·that절을 진주어로 뒤로 보낸다.

triggers.ko: It is + 형용사 + to V — to부정사 진주어 패턴
triggers.en: 'It is + adjective + to V' is the to-infinitive extraposition pattern.

triggers.ko: It is + 형용사 + that 절 — that 진주어 패턴
triggers.en: 'It is + adjective + that-clause' is the that-extraposition pattern.

triggers.ko: It is necessary that + 주어 + (should) + 동사원형 — 가정법 현재
triggers.en: 'It is necessary that + S + (should) + base verb' uses present subjunctive.

tools.common_mistake_example: To learn English is important it. (X) → It is important to learn English. (O)

tools.name: 등위접속사 평행 구조
tools.tool_proposition: and/but/or 로 연결된 항목은 같은 품사·구조여야 한다 (명사-명사, V-ing-V-ing, to V-to V).

triggers.ko: and/but/or 양쪽이 같은 품사·구조여야 한다
triggers.en: Items joined by and/but/or must share the same part of speech and structure.

triggers.ko: V-ing and V-ing / to V and to V — 동사 형태 일치
triggers.en: Use 'V-ing and V-ing' or 'to V and to V' — match the verb form.

triggers.ko: not only A but also B — A·B 같은 구조
triggers.en: 'Not only A but also B' requires A and B to be parallel structures.

tools.common_mistake_example: I like swimming and to run. (X) → I like swimming and running. (O)
